title: Does Shinji speak English
wikitext:
[]: Does Shinji speak English?             Quote Post by Oral Stage » Mon Aug 15, 2011 4:41 pm When Mari parachutes onto Shinji in 2.22, can Shinji understand/eavesdrop on her mobile phone conversation? There is so much English in Eva (in Shinji's entry plug, for example), which could mean that we are meant to deduce that Shinji understands Mari's conversation. Thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by Tarnsman » Mon Aug 15, 2011 4:50 pm Students in Japan are taught English now, maybe in the Rebuild-verse they are too. If it's important I'm sure it will be touched upon in the upcoming movies.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Aug 15, 2011 5:03 pm Based on this image from right after Mari's phone conversation: (cropped) It looks like Shinji's confused. This isn't to say Shinji didn't get taught some rudimentary English in school. Like Tarnsman mentioned, it's taught in Japanese schools currently, but that doesn't mean they'd be able to follow a spoken conversation, There's a Chinese slang for this phenomenon called "deaf-mute English" - can't speak it nor understand it, but can read and write it with a relateively high level of competence. This is also why English tutors are sought after, because you don't really get good at speaking and undrestanding spoken English unless your teacher is really good at it.

[]:             Quote Post by Tarnsman » Mon Aug 15, 2011 5:06 pm Ornette wrote: Based on this image from right after Mari's phone conversation: (cropped) It looks like Shinji's confused.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Aug 15, 2011 5:15 pm Compare it to a more "surprised" look before and after the English conversation, which she's speaking Japanese. Though admittedly, before Mari started speaking English, Shinji looked like he was in a bit of pain.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon Aug 15, 2011 5:22 pm I doubt strongly that we're meant to assume he got any meaningful information out of the phone call. Shinji has these sporadic episodes of density anyway, and many Japanese don't follow spoken English well.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Aug 15, 2011 7:17 pm Azathoth wrote: I doubt strongly that we're meant to assume he got any meaningful information out of the phone call. Shinji has these sporadic episodes of density anyway, and many Japanese don't follow spoken English well.

[]:             Quote Post by Rodeo » Mon Aug 15, 2011 9:48 pm Shinji is definitely onto Mari's little games. He's like a panther.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Mon Aug 15, 2011 10:20 pm If (the Japanese version) of Yu Yu Hakusho is anything to go by, Japanese people do learn English, but the result is incredibly off the chart and sounds nothing close to the real thing. I doubt their comprehension is any better, especially for 14-year-olds who have just started high school. Or maybe it's just like in the rest of Animeland where language doesn't matter.

[]:             Quote Post by cd603 » Tue Aug 16, 2011 3:06 am Rodeo wrote: Shinji is definitely onto Mari's little games. He's like a panther.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Tue Aug 16, 2011 3:26 pm ^bwhahahahaha, NO but that would funny if he tried to be Mr. Sex Panther with Mari in 3.0 or 4.0

[]:             Quote Post by Oral Stage » Tue Aug 16, 2011 4:23 pm I doubt that Mari gave Shinji a second thought when she answered the phone. She switched to English because that's what the person on the other end speaks.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Aug 17, 2011 6:11 pm Oral Stage wrote: I doubt that Mari gave Shinji a second thought when she answered the phone. She switched to English because that's what the person on the other end speaks.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Aug 17, 2011 6:40 pm Kendrix wrote: But no matter how much of the conversation he understood, he's only heard one side of it, most of it was about how she didn't land where she wanted to, and he was probably too baffled at her simple existance/the surreality of the situation, to give much thought to what she's saying, so it's extremely unlikely that he extracted any significant information from that.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Aug 19, 2011 8:06 pm Azathoth wrote: The obvious parallel is to Asuka's phone call in ep23, where Shinji is similarly eavesdropping on a girl speaking a foreign language and, though he sits there and listens to the whole thing, receives no real information from it (apart from "gee i sure wish i had a family"). Really, he barely seems to be paying attention to her as much as he's just going "wut"
summary:
[]: Does Shinji speak English?             Quote Post by Oral Stage » Mon Aug 15, 2011 4:41 pm When Mari parachutes onto Shinji in 2.22, can Shinji understand/eavesdrop on her mobile phone conversation? There is so much English in Eva (in Shinji's entry plug, for example), which could mean that we are meant to deduce that Shinji understands Mari's conversation. Thoughts?

[]:
